featured.curatorial_note: The Bowden Legacy represents a unique opportunity to acquire memorabilia tied to American football royalty, blending sports history with personal family artifacts in a two-day event.

featured.title: Lifetime Collections:Local&International Art
featured.curatorial_note: A lifetime of curated international and local art collecting now comes to market—this Birmingham estate sale promises museum-quality pieces and diverse artistic traditions under one roof.

featured.title: Dick Lewis' Personal Automotive Collection Estate Auction

"World Known Flathead Ford & Hot Rod Builder Dick Lewis' of Lititz PA - 70+ Year Personal Collection" Estate Auction - Classic Auto Parts- Many Flat Head Ford and Classic Chevy Parts -Ranging from 1930's-1970's, Tools & Equipment, Antiques, Automotive Memorabilia, Advertising & More!
 
This is an extraordinary opportunity to purchase items from this Single Owner Lifetime Collection
featured.curatorial_note: Dick Lewis's legendary 70-year personal collection of flathead Ford and hot rod parts represents the lifework of a world-renowned builder—an essential trove for serious automotive enthusiasts.

featured.title: Vintage Pin-Up Art, Playboy  & Mid Century Americana
featured.description:
Vintage Pin-Up Art, Playboy Collectibles & Mid-Century Americana

A rare and extensive single-collection offering spanning decades of American illustration art and men's magazine history — a must-see event for serious collectors.

Varga & Petty Originals: Dozens of framed works by Alberto Vargas and George Petty, the undisputed masters of mid-century pin-up illustration. Esquire calendar girls, double-sided Varga Varieties of 1946, and large-format framed prints up to 47" — museum-quality pieces at auction prices

Golden Age Playboy Magazines: Rare early issues including October 1955, May 1957, August 1957, and multiple copies from 1958–1962 — the true golden era of Hugh Hefner's vision. Also featured: the legendary August 1965 issue signed by cover model Jo Collins, 1960s runs of 39+ issues, and sought-after celebrity editions featuring Madonna, Marilyn Monroe (7 January 1997 editions), Anna Nicole Smith (9 copies), Bo Derek, and a Playboy: The Parody issue.

Ephemera & Advertising Art: Zippo and novelty lighters (14+), vintage Mennen advertising framed prints, metal advertising signs, pin-up ashtrays, matchbooks, coasters, and steering wheel spinners — perfect for man-cave or mid-century modern décor.Marilyn Monroe Collection: Knives, porcelain dolls, ornaments, playing cards, photographs, and calendars dedicated to the ultimate American icon.

Sealed & Graded-Condition Items: Sealed binder sets of Playboy Centerfold Collector Cards, magazines still in original shipping plastic, and pristine 1970s–1990s runs totaling 500+ issues.

Whether you're a Vargas illustration collector, vintage men's magazine archivist, mid-century Americana enthusiast, or advertising art investor, this auction delivers extraordinary depth .Preview available by appointment only on Friday 3-6-26 call 317-468-3705

*** All items sold as-is, all sales are final.

*** Shipping on ITEMS ONLY****NO FRAMES, CASES OR DISPLAYS WILL BE SHIPPED all items will be removed from frames, cases or displays and they will be disposed of.
featured.curatorial_note: An extensive single-collector assemblage of vintage pin-up art and Playboy memorabilia spanning decades—a rare and comprehensive window into mid-century American illustration and popular culture.

featured.title: 03-15-2026 Important Fine Art Auction
featured.description:
Important Fine Art Auction!

Online Auction  7PM Sunday, March 15, 2026
Bid Now! www.tomhallauctions.com

This curated online auction presents an outstanding collection of fine art that spans mediums,
movements, and generations of artistic mastery. The offering includes oil, acrylic, and watercolor
paintings alongside sculptures, expressive sketches, pencil and charcoal works, pastels, and
richly layered mixed media pieces. Both framed and unframed works are available, allowing
collectors flexibility in presentation and investment. Styles range from Impressionistic and
abstract compositions to realism, still life, landscape, and beyond, creating a dynamic and
visually compelling catalog.

Highlights include works by William Hugh Ferguson, celebrated for his refined academic
technique and sensitive portraiture, with a special emphasis on his beautifully executed
sketches that reveal his mastery of form, light, and human expression. These works on paper
offer a rare glimpse into Ferguson’s creative process, capturing movement and emotion with
remarkable economy and precision. The auction also features Western watercolor paintings by
Allen M. Dean, known for his atmospheric depictions of frontier life and expansive landscapes
that embody the spirit of the American West.

Sculptural works by internationally recognized artist Steve Tobin bring bold organic forms and
powerful presence, while pieces by Aldro T. Hibbard reflect the rich tradition of American
Impressionism with luminous color and dynamic brushwork. Works by Zvi Mairovich offer
striking contemporary abstraction with energetic texture and movement, complemented by the
refined modernist sensibilities of Klaus Ihlenfeld. The collection further includes compelling
works by Stephen George Maniatty, Hans Moller, and noted American painter William Langson
Lathrop, whose landscapes helped shape early 20th-century Impressionism in the United
States.

Rounding out the auction is a finely detailed KPM hand-painted portrait plaque, representing the
renowned German porcelain tradition prized for its craftsmanship and artistic excellence.
Together, this diverse and thoughtfully assembled collection offers something for every collector,
from museum-quality names to expressive works full of character and history. Whether you are
seeking a timeless classic, a striking contemporary piece, or rare artist studies, this auction
provides an exceptional opportunity to acquire beautiful and meaningful art.

Aldro Thompson Hibbard (American, 1886–1972)
Prominent Rockport School painter celebrated for bold impasto New England winter landscapes and one of the leading Massachusetts Impressionists.

Steve Tobin (American, b. 1957)
Internationally recognized contemporary sculptor known for monumental bronze, glass, and steel works exploring nature and growth forms, including his well-known Root series exhibited in museums and public spaces worldwide.

William Langson Lathrop (American, 1859–1938)
Important founding member of the New Hope Art Colony; American Impressionist known for atmospheric Pennsylvania river landscapes and seasonal rural views.

Hans Moller (American, 1857–1925)
German-born American painter active in Philadelphia and New Hope circles, recognized for Tonalist landscapes and coastal scenes with moody, subdued palettes.

William H. Ferguson (American, 1850–1923) (over 100 pieces from this artist)
Pennsylvania Impressionist-associated landscape painter active in Philadelphia, noted for atmospheric rural and river scenes influenced by late-19th-century American tonalism.

Klaus Ihlenfeld (German-American, 1934–2005)
Sculptor and designer known for cast-bronze furniture and sculptural forms built from dense, organic clusters resembling reeds and natural growth. Active in the United States in the late 20th century, his work blends modernist design with fine art and is collected as both decorative art and sculpture.

Plus many additional listed and unlisted artists – original oils, lithographs, and framed works – offering collecting opportunities in every price range.

Preview by appointment, call 610-799-0808

Items are located at Tom Hall Auction Gallery, 4644 Rt. 309, Schnecksville, Pa. 18078

Pick up hours at the gallery following the auction:  12noon-3:30pm the day after the auction and Monday thru Friday from 9:30AM-3:30PM  daily.

All items MUST be removed within 7 DAYS of the auction.

Local deliver available. Please call us at 610-799-0808 for information.

Online Only Bidding: Auction starts closing with 2 items closing per minute, bidding continues as auction closes.Extended bidding, if a bid is placed within the last 1 minute of a lot’s scheduled close time the bidding will be extended 1 minute from the last bid.

Buying at auction is not like buying new; the items are used, vintage and antique. They are consigned by estates and people downsizing. They have been used in people’s homes for their purposes, so while most items are in good condition, most are not in perfect condition. We provide private previews for the public to come to the gallery and physically inspect items being sold. If you cannot be here for the inspection, please contact us by email for additional photos or more detailed condition reports.

We try to present items to bidders fairly; however, it is the bidder’s duty to make independent investigation of the condition of items before bidding.

Online bidding Terms:  Major Credit Cards, 20% Buyer’s Premium, 6% Sales Tax collected if you have provided a valid Sales Tax Exemption Certificate. 

IMPORTANT PLEASE READ PAYMENT TERMS FOR THIS AUCTION 

All major credit cards are accepted. Your credit  card will immediatley be charged for all purchases. 

However at auctioneers discretion INVOICES EXCEEDING $1200 may required payment by wire transfer or cash. 

Do You Have Something to Sell? We Can Help!
Real Estate Sales, Downsizing & Estate Services
Business Liquidations, Jewelry, Couture, Motorcycles & Car Collections
Estate Appraisals
Tom Hall Auctions 610-799-0808
featured.curatorial_note: A curated fine art auction spanning mediums and periods—this carefully selected collection reflects serious artistic merit and investment potential for art connoisseurs.

featured.title: March 2026 Online Coin Auction
featured.description:
Gold: 25+ Vintage Gold Pieces - $1, $2.50, %5 & $10 - Both Liberty & Indians

Silver: Over 700 Silver oz. including 600+American Silver Eagles, uncirculated rolls, 200+ Peace & Morgan silver dollars, many AU/BU. $400 plus in 90% silver coins including uncir. Frankling Half set & Partial XF/AU Walking Liberty set and more.

Currency: Rare & Unusual large notes, $1 notes: 1896 Educational, 1890 Star, 1899 Black Eagle, 1862, 1917 & 1923 Red seals, 1923 in High grade blue seal silver certificates. $2 Notes: 1899, 1917 Red seal & 1914 Philly. $5 1902 Columbus, OH National currency note. 1914 $10 Note & 1922 $20 Gold Certificate.

Misc: Wheat pennies, buffalo nickels, several lots of early type coins and more.

Preview: March 10th from 2- 4pm.
Pickup: March 12th & 13th from 9am - 4pm.
featured.curatorial_note: Over 700 ounces of silver including uncirculated American Silver Eagles, Peace dollars, and Morgan dollars, plus 25+ vintage gold pieces—a numismatist's treasure trove.

featured.title: Multi Estate Auction - Lionel Trains - 10c Comics & more
featured.description: This 500 lot auction features a motorcycle, a large selection of Lionel trains, and new found 10 cent golden age comic books.  There is also an assortment of antiques including signed stoneware pcs, a variety of collectibles, tools and household.
featured.curatorial_note: A multi-estate discovery combining 10-cent golden age comic books with pristine Lionel train collections and signed stoneware—nostalgia and investment value converge beautifully.

featured.title: Vinyl Record Auction
featured.description: This online vinyl record auction brings together an exciting collection of  pre-owned albums for music lovers, collectors, and audiophiles alike. Featuring a wide range of genres—including classic rock,, pop, and rare imports—this sale offers something for every taste and era.The catalog includes original pressings, limited editions, colored vinyl, promotional copies, and hard-to-find releases from legendary artists and iconic labels. Many lots feature well-preserved cover art, inserts, and lyric sleeves. Whether you’re searching for a first pressing of a timeless classic or looking to expand your everyday listening collection, this auction delivers both nostalgia and discovery.
featured.curatorial_note: A carefully curated vinyl record collection for music enthusiasts and audiophiles—spanning genres with pre-owned albums that celebrate both sonic history and collecting culture.

3RD ANNUAL WESTERN BONANZA ART & COLLECTIBLES ONLINE AUCTION

AUCTION RUNS : 2/26 - 3/19

PREVIEW TO BE HELD:
3/14 - 10AM - 2PM

PREVIEW & PICK UP HELD AT 219 9TH AVE S 
GREAT FALLS, MT (IN THE ALLEY)

Pick-up times will be:
3/21 - 9AM - 4PM
3/22 - 10AM - 2PM 

STONEWARE CROCKS, BRONZE'S, FRAMED ART, FIREARMS & AMMO, SIGNED BOOKS, WOOD CARVINGS, POTTERY, NATVIE AMERICAN DOLLS, PENDELTON ITEMS, KNIVES, JEWELRY, TAXIDERMY, SADDLE'S, BELT'S & BUCKELS, CLOCKS, FURNITURE, AND SO MUCH MORE…
art.curatorial_note: The third annual Western Bonanza art and collectibles auction promises regional artistry and authentic Americana for collectors nationwide.

art.title: City Steam Memorabilia Online Auction
art.description:
The Legendary City Steam Brewery Restaurant will offer  a large selection of the Framed ART & MEMORABILIA  that hung on the walls in each Bar - Dining Room &  Banquet Room.  This online auction begins NOW  until  MONDAY MARCH 9th at 5pm.  
PICKUP  TUESDAY MARCH 10th 12:00 to 6:00 PM & Wednesday MARCH 11th from 9:00 to Noon. 
(Location Pickup will be on the Main Level of the Restaurant -
 Enter Through the Residence Inn Main Foyer off of Main St.)  

The memorabilia left unsold after the online auction will be sold upon pickup days to registered bidders as first priority.  You will be updated with new postings or Text a Request to Jen  860-280-8610   
Any Bidder who needs to Schedule Pickup Beyond the Pickup Schedule, 
will be charged a handling fee to move the products to a holding room (minimum of $10 per our discretion - please schedule promptly.
art.curatorial_note: City Steam Brewery's framed memorabilia and art collection—iconic pieces that decorated legendary restaurant spaces now available to collectors.

toys.title: LEGO Sets, Minifigures, Accessories (3/15/26)(CO)
toys.description: Sunday, March 15, 2026 online auction of Legos. Almost 200 lots are Star Wars themed, 15 Harry Potter, 10 Batman / Batmobile, other sets includes Porsche, Monster Flighters, Ninjago, Grand Carousel, Disney, Architecture including Great Wall of China, Creator Wiinter, Ideas sets, Jurassic World / Park, Marvel, The Simpsons, Pirates of the Caribbean, Stranger Things, Sci-Fi, and accessories / pieces with instruction booklets and manuals, 37 flattened boxes, and large groupings of bricks and minifigures.
toys.curatorial_note: Nearly 200 Star Wars LEGO lots plus Harry Potter, Batman, and premium sets—a comprehensive auction for serious brick collectors and enthusiasts.

toys.title: March Monthly Farm Toy Auction - Friday
toys.description: Another THREE amazing days of farm toys, trucks, and memoriablia! Friday and Sunday include a variety of brands, while Saturday is ALL ALLIS-CHALMBERS! Let's just say it is a sale you don't want to miss! Join us live in Dyersville, IA, or online! We are happy to ship items worldwide!
toys.curatorial_note: Three days of exceptional farm toy auctions including a dedicated all-Allis-Chalmers day—essential for agricultural memorabilia and diecast collectors.

toys.title: SPRING TOY AUCTION
toys.description:
SPRING TOY AUCTION
FRIDAY- MARCH 13, 2026  6PM
HELD AT:  COUNTRY SIDE EQUIPMENT SALES
5064 KENSINGTON RD NE
CARROLLTON, OH 44615
330-627-5100

1:8 Scale Black International 1066, Farmall Hydro 100, JD 8530 w/ Duals Pedal Tractor, Case IH MX255 Pedal Tractor, White Pedal Tractor Trailer, JD Pedal Tractor Trailer, 1:16 NIB AC6060 2WD w/ Radio, AC D21 60th Anniversary,  Case 5140, Case IH 620 Quad Trac, IH 1206, IH 1256 w/ Mower, Farmall 560 Gold Demonstrator w/ 5X Plow,  JD 4320 Diesel, JD 435, Farm House Set, Custom made Wooden Barns by Keith Thompson, Signs, Winross Semi's, Spider Girl autographed by Joan VanArk, Aaron Rodgers "POP" Collector, Star Wars Pop Collectors, Locking Bank Trucks.
Many more than listed.  These are the collections of 6 different individuals.  Many are new in the box.  Bid in person- save the online buyers premium or view catalog and bid online at countrysidecarrollton.hibid.com .  Consignments are still being taken until March 2nd for this auction. Please call ahead.
Terms:  Cash/ Check or Credit Card with 3.5% conveyance fee.  Onsite buyer's premium is $1 per lot.  Preview will be during normal business hours of 9am to 4pm or Friday- March 13th after 1PM.  Pick Up will be Saturday March 14 during our equipment auction or Monday March 16. 9am to 5PM.  
Michael A. Lozier, Auctioneer
toys.curatorial_note: Spring toy auction featuring 1:8 scale farm equipment and vintage tractors—perfect for collectors of agricultural memorabilia and die-cast models.
